title: centaurium fastigiatum
intro: Centaurium fastigiatum, also known as Kuckuckslichtnelke or tall centaury, is a perennial plant species in the gentian family, characterized by its tall spikes of yellowish-green flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of iridoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Centaurium fastigiatum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders, such as dyspepsia and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as its possible anti-anxiety and anti-depressant effects. Centaurium fastigiatum is typically prepared as a tincture, tea, or dried extract, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote digestive health and well-being.
